law: Before you run the sample code project in Xcode, ensure you’re using macOS 14.0 or later.
Use a variety of C++ APIs in Swift – and vice-versa – across multiple targets and frameworks in an Xcode project.
Before you run the sample code project in Xcode, ensure you’re using macOS 14.0 or later.
Use C++ APIs in Swift – and Swift APIs in C++ – in a single framework target, and consume the framework’s APIs in a separate app target.
Make sure that the complication appears on the active watch face.
Each target generates warnings that must be resolved.
Use this code to follow along with a guide to migrating your code to take advantage of the full concurrency protection that the Swift 6 language mode provides.
Use distributed actors to take your Swift concurrency and actor-based apps beyond a single process.
You don’t have to convert all of your code all at once; instead, you can use specific refactoring techniques to convert your code one piece at time.
Make sure the Coffee Tracker complication appears on the active watch face.
You can remove completion handlers by updating these calls to use the - versions.
In some cases an SDK call requires using a completion handler.

To the results of a completion handler in these cases, add a : To protect the stored properties on the controller when accessed asynchronously, change from a type to an : Calls to functions from synchronous functions are made by creating new asynchronous tasks, which can use to wait for completion:

To ensure that all updates to this property are made on the main thread, place the type on the main actor: Two methods that perform synchronous IO — the and methods — are factored out into a separate actor, which performs these activities away from the main thread.
The model type on the main actor must use to call methods on the actor, which allows other work to run on the main thread during the synchronous IO operations.
Calls to the object from SwiftUI views don’t require any use of as these views are also on the main actor due to their use of .
